description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the docker:latest image with the docker:dind service and set DOCKER_DRIVER=overlay and GIT_DEPTH=3. Define a single stage named build. Add a job called docker-build prod that runs only on tags, is manual, does not allow failure, and executes the script: docker build --pull -t $CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME:rootfs ., mkdir -p ./plugin/rootfs, docker create --name tmp $CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME:rootfs, docker export tmp | tar -x -C ./plugin/rootfs, cp config.json ./plugin/, docker rm -vf tmp, docker plugin create n0r1skcom/$CI_PROJECT_NAME:$CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME ./plugin, docker plugin create n0r1skcom/$CI_PROJECT_NAME:latest ./plugin, docker login -u $DOCKER_USER -p $DOCKER_PASSWD, docker plugin push n0r1skcom/$CI_PROJECT_NAME:$CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME, docker plugin push n0r1skcom/$CI_PROJECT_NAME:latest. Also add a job called docker-build test that runs on branches, is manual, does not allow failure, and runs the same script except it creates only the versioned plugin and pushes only that one. Both jobs share the same before_script that echoes $CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME.
